concepts.name: Matrix Definition
concepts.short_definition: A rectangular array of numbers, symbols, or expressions, arranged in rows and columns.

concepts.name: Matrix Addition
concepts.short_definition: Combining two matrices of the same dimensions by adding their corresponding elements.

concepts.name: Scalar Multiplication
concepts.short_definition: Multiplying every element of a matrix by a single number (scalar).

concepts.name: Matrix Multiplication
concepts.short_definition: A binary operation that produces a new matrix from two matrices, with specific rules for element calculation.

concepts.name: Identity Matrix
concepts.short_definition: A square matrix with ones on the main diagonal and zeros elsewhere.

concepts.name: Zero Matrix
concepts.short_definition: A matrix where all elements are zero.

concepts.name: Transpose of a Matrix
concepts.short_definition: A new matrix formed by interchanging the rows and columns of the original matrix.

concepts.name: Matrix Inverse
concepts.short_definition: For a square matrix, another matrix that, when multiplied by the original, results in the identity matrix.

concepts.name: Determinant of a Matrix
concepts.short_definition: A scalar value that can be computed from the elements of a square matrix and encodes certain properties of the linear transformation described by the matrix.

concepts.name: Augmented Matrix
concepts.short_definition: A matrix formed by appending the columns of two given matrices, usually to represent the coefficients and constants of a system of linear equations.
